assignment: Ledger paths: verify collab protocol paths, env vars, and monitor consumption match on-disk layout; identify drift.
summary: Collab ledger paths are structurally consistent across protocol.json, env vars (all 5 agent scripts), and on-disk layout. Four classes of drift identified: (1) squad-leads/ vs squads/ directory name split, (2) reviews.jsonl missing evidence field in 11/18 lines, (3) consensus.jsonl line 5 missing evidence, (4) broadcasts.jsonl line 3 missing evidence. Monitor uses relative path resolution which is CWD-dependent.

path_alignment.details: All 7 COLLAB env vars (STAMPEDE_COLLAB_DIR, STAMPEDE_COLLAB_PROTOCOL, STAMPEDE_COLLAB_PROPOSALS, STAMPEDE_COLLAB_REVIEWS, STAMPEDE_COLLAB_IMPROVEMENTS, STAMPEDE_COLLAB_CONSENSUS, STAMPEDE_COLLAB_BROADCASTS) match protocol.json ledger paths exactly and match on-disk files in all 5 commander agent scripts.

monitor_path_consumption.details: stampede-monitor.sh resolves BASE via fallback chain: explicit arg -> .stampede/ (CWD-relative) -> /Users/greggcochran/.stampede/ -> /Users/greggcochran/.copilot/stampede/. Monitor then uses /collab/ for all ledger file paths. When invoked from repo root, this resolves correctly to the same collab/ directory. However, if invoked from any other CWD, the monitor will use a different (non-existent) path and silently skip the collab summary (guarded by [[ -d /collab ]] && return 0 at line 42). No env var consumption of STAMPEDE_COLLAB_* vars in monitor; it constructs paths independently from BASE. This is a latent path drift risk.
monitor_path_consumption.monitor_path_construction: local collab_dir="/collab" (not using STAMPEDE_COLLAB_DIR env var)
monitor_path_consumption.risk: Monitor ignores STAMPEDE_COLLAB_DIR env var entirely; constructs path independently. If BASE misresolves, collab summary is silently skipped.

drift_findings.description: squad-leads/ vs squads/ naming split for commander-002/sq-026
drift_findings.detail: commanders/commander-002/squad-leads/sq-026/ contains worker-prompts.json (launcher artifact). commanders/commander-002/squads/sq-026/atoms/ is the worker result-write path (runtime artifact). Two separate directories represent the same squad lead. The launcher (stampede.sh) writes to squad-leads/ but worker result paths use squads/. No single canonical path for squad lead data.
drift_findings.note: squads/ has 9 entries; squad-leads/ has only 3. The two sets overlap at sq-026 but are not the same population.

drift_findings.description: reviews.jsonl: 11 of 18 lines missing required evidence field
drift_findings.detail: protocol.json required_fields includes evidence. peer_review event entries in lines 1-11 omit evidence and instead include verdict and target_commander fields. Lines 12-18 do include evidence. This indicates two different writer implementations with inconsistent schemas for the peer_review event type.

drift_findings.description: consensus.jsonl line 5 missing required evidence field
drift_findings.detail: Line 5 has fields: ts, run_id, commander_id, event, item_id, summary, verdict, confidence, based_on, source_refs. Missing evidence. Lines 1-4 and 6-8 are compliant. Likely a different commander wrote line 5 using a consensus schema variant.

drift_findings.description: broadcasts.jsonl line 3 missing required evidence field
drift_findings.detail: Line 3 has fields: ts, run_id, commander_id, event, item_id, summary, confidence, adopted_items, source_refs. Missing evidence and consensus_item_id. This appears to be an adoption-summary broadcast (different semantic than a standard broadcast) written by a commander using a non-standard schema variant.

no_drift: All 5 STAMPEDE_COLLAB_* env vars are identical across all 5 commander agent scripts.
protocol.json ledger paths match env vars exactly (absolute paths).
All 6 collab files declared in protocol.json exist on disk.
proposals.jsonl (5 lines) and improvements.jsonl (11 lines) are fully compliant with required_fields schema.
Monitor collab_dir derivation from BASE is internally consistent.
JSONL append-only format is maintained; no truncation or rewrite artifacts detected.

recommendations: Consolidate squad-leads/ and squads/ into a single canonical directory (suggest: squad-leads/) to eliminate path ambiguity.
Add evidence field enforcement to peer_review event writers or update protocol.json required_fields to separate required_fields by event type.
Have stampede-monitor.sh consume STAMPEDE_COLLAB_DIR env var when available instead of constructing path independently from BASE.
Document that reviews.jsonl uses two schema variants for peer_review; update protocol.json or fix writer implementations.
